reason: Original M2 requirements source: provider runtime contract, AI SDK bridge boundary, model events, routing, usage/error normalization, tool intent, and model hooks.
Cross-project provider abstraction evidence and Guga migration recommendations for SDK isolation, model registry, routing, usage/cost, and transport boundaries.
Project LLM integration roadmap that frames contract-first provider boundary, streaming event adapter, capability adaptation, and error recovery stages.
Product architecture direction: small core, plugin ecosystem, provider SDKs outside core, events as facts, runtime-owned permissions and hooks.
Backend package boundaries: keep real provider SDKs and real tools out of packages/core; plugin/provider packages belong outside core.
Core runtime quality bar, forbidden provider SDK leakage, required tests, and pnpm verification commands.
Structured observable runtime error patterns to extend for provider error taxonomy, retry/fallback, hook failures, and model call failures.
M2 crosses core contracts, plugin bridge, router, events, hooks, and tests; implementers must map data flow and boundaries explicitly.
Use existing registry, event bus, hook kernel, plugin host, and test fixture patterns instead of duplicating runtime infrastructure.
